title: Distimake glaber
taxonomy: Distimake glaber is classified as a species of plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory or bindweed family. This classification is based on its characteristics and morphological features, such as its twining stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. The species Distimake glaber is native to parts of Asia, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats, including forests and grasslands. This classification is used by botanists and taxonomists to identify and understand the relationships between different plant species.
